methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Abridge is a Series E-stage biotech and health company seeking valuation in a sector containing 3 public comparables and 8 private peers at similar valuation tiers. The target's last post-money valuation stands at $5.3B following $1.2B+ in cumulative raise.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $5.3B Series E post-money, implying $7.4B. Peer-median analysis across 10 same-sector comparables—including public companies Tempus AI ($6.0B) and Freenome ($2.6B), alongside private peers Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), and Insitro ($2.4B)—yields a median of $2.3B with a range of $1.5B to $6.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 6.6x across RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) is not applicable due to undisclosed revenue. The wide divergence between stage-progression and peer-median reflects valuation dispersion within the biotech frontier-tech space.

Triangulation across applicable methods supports a range of $2.0B (low) to $8.5B (high), with a median of $4.9B. The current $5.3B valuation sits within this range but above the peer-median, suggesting premium pricing relative to comparable stage and sector peers. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers and the applicability of public-market multiples to a private frontier-tech operator.
